content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W14 (2026-03-30 ~ 2026-04-05) This week, three main tasks were carried out simultaneously: preparation for the MIHD multi-modal spatial transcriptomics paper (5 days, DCC); completion of the TEDDY full-embedding benchmark, Leiden comparison experiments, HD fusion evaluation, and writing of a bilingual paper framework. The research focus was established as “zero-shot FM embedding fusion” with differentiated studies. Additionally, systematic fixes were made to TokenMonitor Linux FloatBall over 5 days. After multiple iterations, the issue evolved from symptom-based fixes to a fixed-window + GDK Input Shape architecture重构. All 21 code review issues in PR #4 were also resolved. The Life-Copilot Discord Bot was completely reimplemented over 2 days, resulting in a net reduction of 2823 lines of code. A multi-CLI agent architecture was introduced, and new features such as screen time tracking, Lark input channels, and MCP tool integration were implemented. Furthermore, research on 63 papers from the NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track and setup of a new Linux workstation environment were completed.
Weekly Overview Indicator Value Date Range 2026-03-30 ~ 2026-04-05 Active Days 7 / 7 Total Conversations 29 Projects Involved 12 Tasks Completed 44 Ongoing Tasks 9 Total Tokens 328,660,991 Total Cost $218.53 Claude Code Tokens 273,046,863 Claude Code Costs $189.80 Codex Tokens 55,614,128 Codex Costs $28.73 Average Daily Cost $31.22 Project Progress MIHD Multi-modal Spatial Transcriptomics Paper (5 active days) — 🔄 Active Completed:
Completed extraction of TEDDY-G 400M full-embedding data for 11 slices and multi-strategy evaluations. It was confirmed that the STAIG framework is highly robust for gene encoders (Teddy ARI=0.495 vs PCA ARI=0.497). Integrated Leiden clustering (binary search matching n_clusters) and STHD pathology GT, completed full-Leiden comparison on DLPFC (9/9 groups). The key conclusion was that high-quality embeddings are superior to clustering methods (STAIG+mclust=0.546 vs STAIG+Leiden=0.513). Completed evaluation of 13 HD fusion strategies. Key negative findings included: simple fusion (concat ARI=0.191) being inferior to single-modal PCA (ARI=0.351), and adaln crashing on large-scale HD data (ARI=0.003). Established the core narrative framework for the paper (Setting=No system study on zero-shot FM embedding fusion/Finding=Fusion Strategies > Simple Fusion is a Trap, Spatial Graph Learning is Key/Application=Cross-slice Retrieval). A complete bilingual paper framework was created by referring to Yuan et al. 2024 Nature Methods. Created 4 GPU Slurm job scripts and 3 paper analysis scripts (cross-slice query aggregation, figure visualization, HD Marker Gene visualization). The docs/ folder was reorganized, and 12 expired scripts were archived. Blocked Issues:
⚠️ Basic contrastive HD fusion experiment is still running (48h limit). The HD learning-based fusion result set is not complete. ⚠️ The root cause of the 151676 slice STAIG model collapse has not been thoroughly analyzed. It is currently handled by skipping it. TokenMonitor FloatBall Linux Adaptation (5 active days) — 🔄 Active Completed:
Fixed Wayland positioning issues in the Linux main window (forcing GDK_BACKEND=x11 to fallback to XWayland). Completed all 21 code review fixes in PR #4: assert!() → Result propagation, SSH lock across asynchronous boundaries, path traversal whitelist protection, I/O error differentiation, Svelte 5 double fetch, FloatBall listener race conditions. There were zero Rust warnings and 199 front-end tests passed. Identified the root cause of GTK size negotiation issues (WebKitGTK natural size causing set_size() to be ineffective). This was resolved by using with_webview() to call widget.set_size_request(). Also, deferred repositioning, multi-monitor misjudgment, pointerdown race conditions, and scroll bars were fixed. Completed the fixed-window + GDK Input Shape architecture重构: The window permanently maintained an expanded size. Cairo::Region was used to control the click-through area. Approximately 150 lines of probe/corrective patch code were removed. 284 Rust+205 front-end tests passed. Organized git history (git reset –soft compressed 24 commits into 2 semantic commits) and pushed it to GitHub. A new Linux workstation version v0.6.1 was built (deb/rpm/AppImage). Blocked Issues:
⚠️ The fixed-window + GDK Input Shape solution needs to be verified on actual devices. ⚠️ There are architectural constraints regarding Linux WM clamping. The correct solution is to fix it with a fixed large size and internal CSS. This needs to be fully verified. Life-Copilot Discord Bot (2 active days) — 🔄 Active Completed:
Completely reimplemented 3170 lines of complex intent routing into a four-layer architecture consisting of CLIAdapter+SessionManager+MCP Server (19 tools) + Skills (4). A net reduction of 2823 lines was achieved, with 419 tests passed. Implemented Screen Time Tracker (GNOME Introspect D-Bus → xprop → cmdline three-level downgrade, AI application classifier, independent asyncio.Task sampling loop, 26/26 tests passed). Integrated Lark Bot for quick idea capture (daemon thread asyncio bridge solved lark-oapi blocking issues, 10 categories of automatic classification). Added Codex/Anthropic backend. The fallback chain was extended to 11 levels. Fixed two overlapping problems in MCP tool calls: Claude -p requires –mcp-config and –permission-mode bypassPermissions. Added structlog structured logging + complete dialogue JSONL audit. Blocked Issues:
⚠️ Accessing OpenReview reviewers’ comments is blocked due to a general 403 error in the API. The OPENREVIEW_USERNAME/OPENREVIEW_PASSWORD environment variables are required. New Linux Workstation Environment Setup (2 active days) — ✅ Completed Completed:
Completed the full system initialization (NVIDIA driver confirmation, Miniconda, SSH key migration of 7 files, TinyProxy proxy, GitHub SSH verification). Verified that RTX 5090 has BF16=220 TFLOPS/s. Configured gadget rclone for two-way synchronization (gdrive remote, covering four types of data: summarize/research/website/test). Installed Claude Code skills and 12 plugins. Research on NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track (1 active day) — ✅ Completed Completed:
Analyzed 63 papers under the gadget research scout framework. Deeply analyzed 5 top papers, generating a three-dimensional report on writing structure/ publication elements/knowledge extraction, and a comprehensive writing guide. Identified 3 actionable research directions: multi-dimensional evaluation benchmark for VLM credibility, TTA and fine-tuning synergistic effect benchmarks, online continuous learning timing benchmarks. Key Tasks ✅ TokenMonitor PR #4 All 21 Code Review Fixes (April 02) — System fixes were carried out according to CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM priorities: changing assert!() to Result propagation, SSH lock across asynchronous boundaries (Clone released in advance), path traversal whitelist protection, I/O error differentiation, Svelte 5 double fetch, FloatBall listener race conditions, NaiveDate overflow. There were zero Rust compilation warnings and 199 front-end tests passed. ✅ Life-Copilot Discord Bot Complete Reimplementation (April 04) — Replaced the complex intent routing with a four-layer architecture consisting of CLIAdapter+SessionManager+MCP Server + Skills. Supports Claude/Codex/Gemini CLI fallback chains. The Skills JSON protocol triggers Discord UI decoupling. A net reduction of 2823 lines was achieved, with 419 tests passed. ✅ TokenMonitor FloatBall Linux GTK Size Negotiation Fix (April 04) — Identified the root cause of WebKitGTK natural size causing set_size() to be ineffective. Resolved this issue by using with_webview() to call widget.set_size_request(). Also, deferred repositioning, multi-monitor expansion direction misjudgment, pointerdown race conditions, GTK ScrolledWindow scroll bars, and two-stage collapse were fixed. All tests passed. 🔄 TokenMonitor FloatBall Linux Fixed-Window + GDK Input Shape Architecture Reimplementation (April 05) — Reorganized the float ball from a dynamic resize architecture to a fixed expanded-size window + GDK input_shape_combine_region control for click-through. Eliminated the systematic WM +305px offset. Approximately 150 lines of probe/corrective patch code were removed. 284 Rust+205 front-end tests passed. Waiting for final runtime verification. ✅ MIHD Paper Narrative Line Organization and Bilingual Framework Creation (April 03) — Established the research focus as “zero-shot FM embedding fusion” with a three-layer narrative structure: Simple Fusion is a Trap → Spatial Graph Learning is the Bridge → Correct Fusion Transcends Single Modal. A complete bilingual paper framework was created by referring to Yuan et al. 2024 Nature Methods (including Abstract/Introduction/Results R1-R6/Discussion/Methods). ✅ Leiden vs KMeans Full-Embedding Comparison Experiments (April 01) — Completed comparison of Leiden clustering on 9/9 combinations. The key conclusion was that STAIG+mclust(0.546) > STAIG+Leiden(0.513). Leiden significantly improved weak embeddings (+29%), but had little additional benefit for strong embeddings (+6%). This established the core argument that high-quality embeddings are superior to clustering methods. ✅ MIHD Fig 1-6 Framework Planning (March 31) — Referred to MISO, STAIG, QueST, and Yuan’s papers to plan Fig1=framework, Fig2=DLPFC benchmark, Fig3=Ablation, Fig4=HD application, Fig5=Cross-slice Query, and Fig6=Query bio-discovery. ECL YAML and plans.md were written. ✅ Life-Copilot Screen Time Tracker Full Implementation (April 04) — Planned a 9-stage ccplan + Critic adversarial review (pre-detected Wayland compatibility risks). Implemented GNOME Introspect D-Bus → xprop → cmdline three-level downgrade window tracking. AI application classifier (100+ pre-settings + persistent cache). Independent asyncio.Task sampling loop. Passed 26/26 tests. ✅ Life-Copilot Discord Bot MCP Tool Call Fixes (April 05) — Fixed two overlapping issues: Claude -p requires explicit –mcp-config to load project-level MCP configuration; non-interactive mode requires –permission-mode bypassPermissions. The –allowedTools wildcard was unreliable due to competition with MCP server initialization. By running the Claude -p command directly, the root cause of permission_denials field issues was identified. ✅ NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track Systematic Paper Research (April 04) — Searched for 63 papers and deeply analyzed 5 top papers (TTA-VLM, DATE-LM, CAB, KG-RAG Pitfalls, DCAD-2000). Generated a three-dimensional analysis report on writing structure/ publication elements/knowledge extraction, and a comprehensive writing guide. Identified 3 actionable research directions. ✅ Teddy + STAIG Fusion Evaluation (Full 11 Slices) (March 30) — Completed evaluation of 10/11 slices (skipped model collapse on 151676 models). Avg ARI=0.495 compared to PCA+STAIG’s 0.497. On NMI, Teddy won 6 out of 10 slices. This revealed the high robustness of the STAIG framework for gene encoders, providing experimental evidence for future encoder replacement strategies. ✅ Life-Copilot Lark Bot + Codex/Anthropic Backend + Full-Linkage Token Verification (April 04) — Implemented ThoughtCaptureService shared service layer + AutoCategorizer (10 categories: Chinese & English) + daemon thread asyncio bridge. Added CodexProvider and AnthropicProvider. The fallback chain was extended to 11 levels. Created key_validator.py to verify all services with real APIs. Fixed Google Calendar scope mismatch issues. ✅ FEAT-003: STHD Ground Truth Integration (March 31) — Added a load_sthd_annotations() function to automatically load 545913 annotations. Combined CE and Tumor (Neoplasm+Non-neoplastic Epithelium→Epithelium). 17502/17502 spots had 100% match rate. 3 valid clusters were identified, providing a GT basis for HD ARI/NMI evaluation. ✅ TokenMonitor Linux Main Window Wayland Positioning Fix (April 02) — Identified the Wayland protocol-level prohibition of client-side absolute position settings. After detecting WAYLAND_DISPLAY in the run() entry, forced GDK_BACKEND=x11 to make the application run via XWayland. 283 Rust tests passed. ✅ MIHD GPU Slurm Scripts and Paper Analysis Scripts Batch Creation (April 02) — Created 4 independent Slurm GPU job scripts (STAIG/contrastive/LLaVA/AdaLN) and 3 Python analysis scripts (cross-slice query aggregation, paper figure visualization, HD Marker Gene visualization). All scripts passed syntax verification. ✅ Life-Copilot Structured Logging and Operation Audit System (April 05) — Replaced stdlib logging with structlog. Implemented dev-color output + prod JSON format + 10MB rotation. Created audit_log.py to write complete dialogue JSONL audits in Discord on_message, FastAPI middleware, and CLISelector.send. Fixed ExtraAdder omission issues. 420 tests passed. ✅ TEDDY-G 400M Full-Embedding Extraction (11 Slices) (March 30) — Extracted 1024d gene embeddings for DLPFC’s 11 slices in the conda teddy environment and cached them in embeddings_cache/gene/teddy/. Total size was 185MB. Also extracted UNI2 staig_strict visual features (256×256 patch, no ImageNet normalization, frequency-domain filtering). 🔄 HD Learning-Based Fusion GPU Experiments (April 01) — staig(ARI=0.258)/llava(ARI=0.201)/adaln(ARI=0.003) were completed. The basic_contrastive experiment is still running (48h limit). Key negative findings included: adaln crashing on large-scale HD data (17502 spots), revealing that data size is a key variable in fusion methods. ✅ docs/ Folder Reorganization and Code Gap Analysis (April 03) — Archived 5 expired documents. Created a three-level structure in docs/paper/figures/. Systemically analyzed the capabilities required by draft_en.md vs existing code. Archived 12 expired scripts. Planned Leiden result recovery scripts, figure generation scripts, spatial continuity metrics, and support for 4 code tasks with multiple seeds. Issues and Solutions 1. GTK bottom-up size negotiation caused FloatBall windows to always be 400x400. Tauri’s set_size() was completely ineffective [TokenMonitor] (April 04)### Next Week’s PlansNext week’s key focus areas are as follows: (1) MIHD paper – Collect the complete HD evaluation table for basic_contrastive HD experiments and advance the code gap tasks (Leiden result restoration script, figure generation script, spatial continuity metrics, multi-seed support). Start generating papers and writing drafts to reach a submission-ready level.
(2) TokenMonitor – Finalize the fixed-window + GDK Input Shape solution on real Linux devices. Confirm that the WM clamp issue has been fully resolved. If verification is successful, submit a PR for merging. Close this long-term task related to Linux FloatBall fixes after nearly two weeks.
(3) Life-Copilot – Obtain OpenReview account credentials to unlock access to NeurIPS review comments. Verify the stability of Screen Time Tracker in production environments. Continuously monitor the performance of the 11-level fallback chain in real-world usage.
(4) Research exploration – Translate the research conclusions from NeurIPS D&B Track into specific experimental designs. Evaluate the feasibility of a multi-dimensional benchmark for assessing VLM credibility. Also follow up on the progress of the AionUI remote SSH access feature request.

content:
Monthly Summary — 2026-03 This month’s work was highly focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics multimodal clustering research project: after completing environment initialization on the DCC HPC cluster, we systematically integrated the TEDDY-G 400M foundation model, Leiden clustering algorithm, and STHD pathology annotation Ground Truth. Multi-encoder benchmarks revealed STAIG framework’s high robustness to gene encoders and the systematic transfer loss of Foundation Models on clustering tasks. The paper’s Fig 1–6 framework was finalized, marking the project’s transition from the experimental phase to the paper-writing phase.
Monthly Overview Metric Value Active Days 4 / 31 Total Conversations 14 Projects Involved 5 Tasks Completed 19 Tasks In Progress 3 Total Tokens 63,798,066 Total Cost $39.58 Daily Average Cost $9.89 Project Progress MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics Multimodal Clustering) (4 active days) — 🔄 active The month began with issue triage and documentation, mid-month saw new encoder integration and bug fixes, and the end of the month brought systematic expansion of evaluation dimensions (Leiden, HD dataset, Ground Truth), culminating in a finalized paper structure with experimental work largely complete. Best ARI ~0.546 (DLPFC), STAIG framework robustness verified; slice 151676 remains an unresolved systematic challenge.
Key Milestones:
Identified root cause of cross-slice retrieval failure (PCA/STAIG coordinate spaces are incomparable per slice; scGPT used as cross-slice encoder) Integrated TEDDY-G 400M and completed full 11-slice embedding extraction (185MB) Multi-encoder STAIG fusion benchmark confirmed framework robustness (Teddy vs PCA ARI difference: 0.002) Integrated Leiden clustering (binary search to match n_clusters), STHD Ground Truth (100% match), and HD ARI/NMI evaluation Paper Fig 1–6 framework finalized, referencing structures from four papers: MISO/STAIG/QueST/Yuan DCC HPC Environment Setup (1 active day) — ✅ completed Completed from-scratch environment initialization in a single day, establishing the foundation for running MIHD experiments on DCC. The main obstacle was that config files do not migrate automatically and had to be reconstructed from existing configs.
Key Milestones:
rclone + sync.py completed Google Drive sync for four data categories Investigated GPU partition resources (scavenger-h200 H200×8 available) Installed cchelper skill Key Achievements This Month Identified root cause of MIHD cross-slice retrieval failure and found a solution (2026-03-23, MIHD) — Confirmed root cause is that PCA/STAIG processes each slice independently, producing incomparable embedding spaces. scGPT, due to its shared coordinate system, is the optimal encoder for cross-slice retrieval (average SL@50=0.416, 100% hit rate), revealing the architectural principle that single-slice clustering and cross-slice retrieval require different encoders. Discovered and fixed 4 critical bugs in extract_teddy() (2026-03-29, MIHD) — Systematic source code review revealed: incorrect Tokenizer class name, wrong model forward parameter name (input_ids→gene_ids), wrong output key (last_hidden_state→outputs[‘cell_emb’]), and missing gene symbol to Ensembl ID mapping. After fixes, the first slice (151508, 4384 spots) was successfully validated. Verified STAIG framework’s high robustness to gene encoders (2026-03-30, MIHD) — Teddy 1024d (ARI=0.495) and PCA 50d (ARI=0.497) performed nearly identically under the STAIG framework, with a gap of only 0.002, demonstrating that STAIG’s spatial graph contrastive learning mechanism dominates clustering performance and the impact of gene embedding quality is greatly diluted. Paper Fig 1–6 framework planning completed (2026-03-31, MIHD) — Referencing four competing papers — MISO (Nat Methods), STAIG (Nat Comms), QueST, and Yuan (Nat Methods) — established a complete paper structure: Fig1=framework diagram, Fig2=DLPFC benchmark, Fig3=ablation, Fig4=HD application, Fig5=cross-slice Query, Fig6=Query biological discovery. Written into ECL YAML and plans.md, marking the project’s official transition to the writing phase. TEDDY-G 400M full 11-slice embedding extraction completed (2026-03-30, MIHD) — Completed 1024d gene embedding extraction for all 11 DLPFC slices (151508–151676) in a dedicated conda teddy environment, totaling 185MB, providing a complete data foundation for multi-encoder comparison benchmarks. STHD Ground Truth integration (100% match rate) (2026-03-31, MIHD) — Added _load_sthd_annotations() function, achieving 17502/17502 spots 100% match via r_big/c_big coordinates; CE+Tumor biological merging (Neoplasm + Non-neoplastic Epithelium → Epithelium) yielded 3 meaningful clusters, providing quantitative Ground Truth for HD evaluation. HD full fusion strategy evaluation revealed naive fusion information dilution (2026-03-31, MIHD) — Completed 10/13 HD fusion strategy experiments; found that simple fusion strategies (concat/mean/element_wise_sum) produced ARI (0.19–0.20) lower than unimodal PCA (0.351), while attention fusion approached PCA (ARI=0.342). UNI2 vision-only achieved the highest NMI (0.387) but lower ARI than PCA, revealing that NMI and ARI can be inconsistent across different datasets. Summarized systematic transfer loss pattern of Foundation Models on clustering (2026-03-30, MIHD) — Teddy gene-only ARI (0.186) underperformed PCA (0.255). Systematic analysis confirmed the root cause: Foundation Models are optimized for disease discriminability (not variance maximization), high-dimensional spaces (1024d) degrade KMeans performance, and training data (dissociated scRNA-seq) differs in distribution from spatial transcriptomics. Leiden clustering binary search integration (+15% ARI) (2026-03-31, MIHD) — Implemented binary search in clustering.py to match target n_clusters (range [0.01, 10], up to 20 iterations), enabling fair comparison between Leiden and KMeans. Leiden improved ARI by ~15% over KMeans on DLPFC, but improvement over STAIG was limited. First structured documentation of MIHD project historical issues (2026-03-23, MIHD) — By consolidating plans.md, RESULTS.md, and diagnostic reports, generated docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md — the first centralized record of best ARI 0.546, root cause of cross-slice retrieval failures, fixed bug inventory, and a 4-phase ablation experiment plan, providing a structured entry point for subsequent research. Recurring Issues 1. Systematic failure on a specific slice (151676) (occurred 3 times) Dates: 2026-03-23, 2026-03-30, 2026-03-31 Root Cause: Slice 151676 has data-specific issues: zero-variance embeddings cause mclust to fail and fall back to KMeans (dragging down the global mean), and model collapse under the STAIG framework cannot be recovered. The root cause remains unidentified, possibly related to anomalous gene expression distribution or spatial structure in this slice. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
2. Pipeline hardcoded configuration inconsistent with slice-specific parameters (occurred 2 times) Dates: 2026-03-30, 2026-03-31 Root Cause: Pipeline global configuration (e.g., top_genes=3000, dataset defaults, image path structure) has implicit divergences from slice-specific config (151676 uses 5000 genes) or dataset-specific structure (HD vs DLPFC image paths), causing cache check false positives (sys.exit) and visual extraction failures. Status: 🔧 Workaround available
3. HPC environment configuration obstacles (conda activation, missing config files) (occurred 2 times) Dates: 2026-03-23, 2026-03-31 Root Cause: Config files do not automatically migrate to new machines (sync.json must be manually reconstructed), and conda initialization on SLURM compute nodes differs from login nodes (requires eval $(conda shell.bash hook) rather than source activate). Both issues stem from a lack of experience with environment migration. Status: ✅ Resolved
4. Cross-session context loss requiring repeated background provision (occurred 2 times) Dates: 2026-03-23, 2026-03-29 Root Cause: Claude’s session isolation mechanism means key research progress (e.g., Teddy test results) is not persisted to the memory system, requiring users to re-provide arXiv links or background information in subsequent sessions. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
Human-AI Collaboration Trends Human-led key insights: 7 AI limitation pattern: Cross-session memory loss: context at critical research milestones is not automatically persisted, requiring users to repeatedly provide background AI limitation pattern: Hardcoded assumptions delay bug discovery: defaults to global config rather than dynamically resolving slice-specific parameters, tracing root causes only after actual errors occur AI limitation pattern: Excessive reliance on cluster resources: unable to proactively judge whether small datasets (17502 spots) require GPU/SLURM, defaulting to job submission AI limitation pattern: Convention assumptions about non-standard APIs: assumed HuggingFace standard interfaces without reading source code, leading to 4 TEDDY API usage errors AI limitation pattern: Inefficient monitoring strategy: background tasks used multi-round sleep+ls polling instead of event-driven mechanisms Improvement direction: Proactively trigger memory persistence at critical research milestones to avoid cross-session context loss Improvement direction: Proactively identify path/parameter dataset-specific risks when dealing with multi-dataset pipelines Improvement direction: Do not assume API conventions for non-standard libraries before reading source code; declare upfront that verification is needed Improvement direction: Provide proactive experiment prioritization suggestions rather than just listing options and waiting for user decisions Improvement direction: Estimate compute requirements based on data scale, distinguishing locally runnable tasks from those requiring the cluster Selected Insights This Month Architecture STAIG framework is highly robust to gene encoders: Teddy 1024d vs PCA 50d under STAIG shows only 0.002 ARI difference, demonstrating that spatial graph contrastive learning dominates clustering performance and the impact of gene embedding quality is greatly diluted. Therefore, the bottleneck for optimizing MIHD clustering performance does not lie in the choice of gene encoder. (Source: 2026-03-30) The optimal encoder for single-slice clustering (STAIG/PCA) differs from the optimal encoder for cross-slice retrieval (scGPT): the comparability of embedding spaces (shared coordinate system) is more critical than local quality. Foundation Models naturally suit cross-slice retrieval due to their fixed representation space output, while PCA processes slices independently, producing incomparable coordinate systems. (Source: 2026-03-23) Foundation Models exhibit systematic transfer loss on cross-domain downstream tasks: TEDDY achieves SOTA on disease classification (ACC=0.72) but underperforms PCA on spatial transcriptomics clustering (ARI 0.186 vs 0.255). Root cause is optimization objective mismatch (discriminative representation vs variance maximization), and high-dimensional space (1024d) is unfavorable for KMeans. (Source: 2026-03-30) Domain Knowledge HVG1500 (direct concatenation of highly variable genes without dimensionality reduction) achieves ARI=0.330 on single slices, outperforming all Foundation Models; Leiden clustering improves ARI by ~15% over KMeans on DLPFC, but improvement over STAIG is limited — STAIG’s advantage comes from spatial graph structure and is relatively independent of the clustering algorithm. (Source: 2026-03-30, 2026-03-31) NMI and ARI show inconsistency on HD data: UNI2 vision-only achieves the highest NMI (0.387) but lower ARI than PCA (0.344 vs 0.351); naive fusion (concat/mean) on HD yields ARI (0.19–0.20) lower than unimodal PCA (0.351), indicating information dilution from simple fusion. Both metrics should be reported together. (Source: 2026-03-31) Tools Single-cell foundation model forward interfaces in the HuggingFace ecosystem vary significantly and standard interfaces cannot be assumed: TEDDY uses gene_ids (not input_ids), GeneTokenizer (not Tokenizer), outputs[‘cell_emb’] (not last_hidden_state), and its vocab uses Ensembl IDs (43808 tokens) rather than gene symbols. Source code must be read to confirm. (Source: 2026-03-29) DCC HPC cluster practical notes: SLURM compute nodes require eval $(conda shell.bash hook) && conda activate to initialize conda (source activate does not work); scavenger-h200 provides H200×8 preemptible nodes; account TRES limits are cpu=100/mem=500G; Glob tends to time out on large HPC filesystems — use exact path search instead. (Source: 2026-03-23, 2026-03-31) Debugging Implicit divergence between slice-specific configuration (e.g., staig num_gene) and pipeline global defaults is a high-risk bug type: cache check logic should dynamically resolve actual configuration rather than hardcoding defaults; multi-dataset pipeline image paths should be explicitly differentiated rather than using a unified data_root/section_id logic. content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W13 (2026-03-23 ~ 2026-03-29) This week’s core work focused on multi-model embedding expansion and issue triage for the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. On March 23, environment initialization was completed on the DCC HPC cluster, and historical challenges in the MIHD project were systematically documented — for the first time structuring the root cause of cross-section retrieval failure (incomparable embedding spaces) and the single-section ARI gap as formal documents. On March 29, four critical bugs in the TEDDY foundation model extraction script were successfully fixed, the first DLPFC section embedding extraction was completed, and batch runs were launched, establishing the foundation for a multi-model comparison benchmark.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-23 ~ 2026-03-29 Active Days 2 / 7 Total Conversations 6 Projects Involved 3 Completed Tasks 6 In-Progress Tasks 1 Total Tokens 18,874,903 Total Cost $11.79 Daily Average Cost $5.89 Project Progress MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics Multimodal Clustering (2 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Systematically reviewed historical issues and persisted them as docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md, including root cause analysis of cross-section retrieval failure Identified root cause of cross-section task failure: PCA/STAIG independent processing produces incomparable embedding coordinate systems; scGPT shared space achieves average SL@50=0.416 Fixed 4 critical bugs in the extract_teddy() script, successfully completed TEDDY embedding extraction for section 151508 (4384 spots) Launched batch TEDDY embedding extraction for the remaining 10 DLPFC sections, establishing a four-model comparison framework: scGPT/Geneformer/C2S/TEDDY Blockers:
⚠️ Single-section ARI remains below target (current 0.546 vs. target 0.60+); 4-phase ablation experiment plan not yet executed ⚠️ Root cause of mclust falling back to KMeans due to zero-variance embedding in section 151676 unknown ⚠️ Batch TEDDY embedding extraction (10 sections) still running as of March 29 DCC HPC Cluster Environment Initialization (1 active day) — ✅ completed Completed:
Configured rclone sync.json; completed sync of 4 data categories (summarize/research/test/website) via sync.py pull Investigated GPU resources: common (RTX 2080), scavenger-gpu (A6000/RTX 5000-6000 Ada), scavenger-h200 (H200×8) Installed cchelper skill to ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/ Key Tasks ✅ Fixed 4 critical bugs in extract_teddy() script (2026-03-29) — Discovered and fixed via systematic source code review: ① incorrect import of Tokenizer changed to GeneTokenizer; ② model forward parameter input_ids changed to gene_ids; ③ output access last_hidden_state changed to outputs[‘cell_emb’]; ④ added gene symbol to Ensembl ID mapping logic. ✅ MIHD project issue review and documentation (2026-03-23) — Reviewed current state of the MIHD project (best ARI 0.546, root cause of cross-section retrieval failure, list of fixed bugs), generated structured issue summary document docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md. ✅ TEDDY paper analysis and architecture evaluation (2026-03-29) — Analyzed arXiv 2503.03485, reviewed TEDDY model architecture (Teddy-G/X two encoding types, 6 variants, up to 400M parameters), evaluated performance on disease classification benchmarks (held-out donor multi-class classification 8% higher than Nicheformer, linear probe far superior to fine-tuning). 🔄 DLPFC embedding batch extraction (2026-03-29) — Extracting 1024-dimensional cell embeddings for 11 DLPFC sections in the teddy conda environment; section 151508 complete, remaining 10 sections still running. Issues and Solutions 1. TEDDY extraction script used a non-existent Tokenizer class and incorrect model API (input_ids, last_hidden_state), making the script completely non-functional [MIHD] (2026-03-29) Solution: Read TEDDY source code to confirm correct API: GeneTokenizer, gene_ids parameter, outputs[‘cell_emb’] output key, and added Ensembl ID mapping (60% of genes in the DLPFC dataset are mappable)
2. MIHD cross-section retrieval almost completely failed (10 out of 14 query directions with SL@50=0) [MIHD] (2026-03-23) Solution: Diagnosed root cause as PCA/STAIG independent processing per section producing incomparable embedding spaces; switched to scGPT embeddings (shared space), achieving average SL@50=0.416 with 100% hit rate
3. TEDDY inference with batch_size=32 caused GPU OOM (32GB VRAM), because each sequence is up to 2048 tokens long [MIHD] (2026-03-29) Solution: Reduced batch_size from 32 to 8, bringing memory usage under control
4. New DCC machine had no ~/.config/gadget/sync.json, preventing sync.py from running [DCC Environment Initialization] (2026-03-23) Solution: Inferred rclone_remote configuration from existing summarize config, manually created sync.json and verified remote directory structure before executing pull
Learnings Domain Knowledge (domain) In cross-section tasks in spatial transcriptomics, the comparability of embedding spaces (shared coordinate system) is more critical than local embedding quality. Foundation models (scGPT/TEDDY) are naturally suited for cross-section retrieval due to their fixed representation spaces; PCA/STAIG independent processing produces incomparable coordinate systems and is not suitable for cross-section tasks. The optimal method for single-section clustering (STAIG) and the optimal method for cross-section retrieval (scGPT) are different; different encoders should be selected for different task objectives, and a unified optimal solution should not be pursued. TEDDY-G (rank-value encoding) outperforms TEDDY-X (binned expression); linear probe far outperforms fine-tuning (RA: 0.56→0.93), indicating high embedding quality but susceptibility to overfitting during fine-tuning; 8% higher than Nicheformer on held-out donor multi-class classification. Tools (tools) Single-cell foundation models in the HuggingFace ecosystem (TEDDY, scGPT, etc.) have significantly different forward interfaces; standard HuggingFace interfaces cannot be assumed. Source code must be read to confirm the gene_ids parameter name and output dict structure. TEDDY’s vocab uses Ensembl IDs (43808 tokens) rather than gene symbols. Architecture (architecture) TEDDY uses rank-value encoding, retaining the top 2048 genes as a token sequence. The sequence length is longer than models like scGPT, resulting in higher VRAM requirements and the need for smaller batch sizes during inference. AI Usage Notes Effective patterns:
✓ Systematic code review (read source → verify API → compare implementation), proactively discovering hidden bugs unknown to the user, saving debugging time ✓ Choosing to confirm rather than guess when requirements are ambiguous (private file sync requirements), avoiding incorrect execution ✓ Persisting verbal analysis results as structured documents (PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md), completing knowledge externalization with user initiative Limitations:
✗ Cross-session memory not persisted: unable to recall previous TEDDY test discussions, requiring users to re-provide background information ✗ PDF parsing failure: unable to process binary content when directly fetching arXiv PDFs, requiring fallback to HTML version ✗ Relies on existing documentation for project summaries; cannot infer undocumented implicit progress or autonomously prioritize ablation experiments Outlook for Next Week Next week’s priorities: ① Confirm completion of batch TEDDY embedding extraction (10 DLPFC sections) and integrate four-model (scGPT/Geneformer/C2S/TEDDY) comparative evaluation results; ② Execute the 4-phase ablation experiments in the MIHD single-section ARI improvement plan, focusing on diagnosing the root cause of zero-variance embeddings in section 151676; ③ Based on multi-model comparison results, decide whether a combined encoder strategy is needed (scGPT for cross-section, STAIG for single-section clustering).

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W12 (2026-03-16 to 2026-03-22) This week, we conducted extensive research and development on two major directions: robot operations and spatial transcriptomics. The Error Recovery Benchmark was completed from design of the collection scheme (RBG grouping with 329 demo budgets) to architectural-level trajectory segmentation reconstruction using InteractionSegmenter. In total, 1,627 training scenarios were generated, covering 148 subtypes, representing a 35% increase in coverage. MIHD spatial transcriptomics achieved full-link implementation for cross-slice embedding and established the zero-shot advantage of scGPT (100% accuracy vs UNI2’s 71%). For π₀.₅, we completed the full-link training of the detection head with a loss of approximately 0.253, and designed five conditioning strategies for Exp5-9. The gadget toolchain completed the reconstruction of the Research Profiler architecture, the construction of a unified deploy staging architecture for websites, and all ECC devices were upgraded to opus+max thinking. This week’s key breakthroughs focused on “finding and solving architectural-level root causes”: issues such as non-comparability in per-section PCA coordinate systems, ambiguity in multi-object target_object, and Flax NNX inheritance vs combination were fundamentally resolved this week.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-16 to 2026-03-22 Active Days 6 / 7 Total Conversations 32 Projects Involved 25 Completed Tasks 55 Ongoing Tasks 5 Total Tokens 491,120,167 Total Cost $388.14 Claude Code Tokens 459,397,404 Claude Code Costs $370.34 Codex Tokens 31,722,763 Codex Costs $17.80 Average Daily Cost $55.45 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark (5 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Designed a system with 5 RBG groups to reduce human demonstration requirements from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings) Implemented a complete 8-file pipeline (recovery_types/recovery_segmenter/collection augmentation script), all 139 unit tests passed Fixed bugs in CompositeBodyObject coordinate transformation (lid position changed from 0.211m to 0.1045m) Diagnosed and fixed false positives in drop_in_transit (min_hold_height increased from 0.85 to 0.93, success rate improved from 10% to 50%) Converted serial scenario generation into 32 worker parallel processing (4 GPUs round-robin), speed increased by about 10 times (973 scenarios/41 minutes) Systematic code review identified 5 types of bug roots, fixed error skill parameters in 24 places (Fix A-G) Architectural-level reconstruction: added InteractionSegmenter to segment trajectories based on object interactions, completely bypassing phase detection defects Fixed three core bugs in Panda gripper action polarity reversal, wrong_object filtering, and drop_with_interaction injection strategy Finally generated 1,627 training scenarios, covering 148 subtypes System attributed 48 ungenerable cases to physical constraints rather than pipeline failures Blocked Items:
⚠️ Three_piece_assembly scan reduced from 23 to 4 subtypes (base fixture was mistakenly selected for get_target_object and InteractionSegmenter), code fixes did not implement due to bwrap sandbox limitations ⚠️ Pi0.5-base LoRA training interrupted due to Slurm interactive job expiration, needs to be resubmitted using sbatch ⚠️ Pick_place data generation stopped when generating 184/2000 records due to Slurm expiration MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed cross-slice embedding diagnostics experiments (5 methods × 14 combinations), confirmed that scGPT’s 100% accuracy far exceeds UNI2’s (71%) and PCA/STAIG’s (0-14%) Generated visual PDFs in batches (5 complete sets + 35 per-layer sub-PDFs), produced bilingual diagnostic reports Rewrote batch effect evaluation metrics using pure Python with sklearn (ASW_batch/batch_entropy/batch_kl/graph_connectivity), no R package dependencies required Implemented HarmonyAligner and JointSTAIGAligner, fixed the key bug where alignment parameters were not injected during evaluate stage in all_aligned mode End-to-end verification: Harmony improved batch_entropy from 0.33 to 0.52, batch_kl from 0.39 to 0.25 Fixed 9 issues in three-way parallel code review for /simplify (OOM prevention with CDist → KDTree, centralized constants like NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS) Fixed fairness issues in STAIG hyperparameter scanning (experiment names did not match actual implementations), established fair benchmarks for PCA+UNI/UNI2 (ARI mean 0.47) Completed analysis scripts for 151,676 samples using Visium scanpy and generated 6 visual charts Blocked Items:
⚠️ Harmony’s improvement on batch effects of PCA embedding was limited (per-section PCA feature spaces were inconsistent and could not be fundamentally solved through post-processing) π₀.₅ / robobrain_pi (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed 5 bugs in vla_infer.py (infinite loop after task completion, None check order crash, idle state not broadcasted) Implemented separate monitoring of action_loss and task_loss (JAX has_aux mechanism, three curves tracked independently by wandb) Completed π₀.₅ task detection head full-link: CompletionHead/Pi0WithCompletionHead (inheritance method)/train_completion_head.py, training started normally with a loss of approximately 0.253 Identified and fixed the root cause of silent discarding of manip_progress field in RobotwinOutputs.call, established a basis for progress evaluation Designed and implemented five conditioning strategies for Exp5-9 (variations for from_hidden/sinusoidal/detach) Fixed LIBERO libero_object_com registration deficiency and multiple evaluation environment blockages Completed data conversion from 5 tasks’ HDF5 to LeRoBot and norm statistics calculation (coffee/stack, total over 8000 records) Blocked Items:
⚠️ Pi0.5-base LoRA training was forcibly terminated due to Slurm interactive job expiration, needs to be resubmitted using sbatch ⚠️ K8s GPU isolation configuration for LIBERO evaluation environment needs to be checked manually each time Gadget Toolchain (Research Profiler + summarize + website) (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Analyzed academic profiles of 20+ researchers in batches, identified 5 serious name conflicts Two rounds of code refactoring: three-level parsing chain (exact ID → paper reverse search → name search), quantitative metrics主导歧义权重, added –author-id CLI parameter Fixed recursive retry deadlock when S2 speed limit was exceeded (changed to exponential backoff with a maximum of 5 attempts) Fixed the root cause of the dead loop in gadget _finalized (3 source logs first exported with finalized=False caused merge never converging) Fixed npx subprocesses hanging permanently under capture_output=True mode (added –yes parameter) ECC devices upgraded to opus + effortLevel: max monthly_summary.py added support for independent Codex usage aggregation Completed Hugo site bugJournal navigation dropdown menu (custom header.html partial covering the theme) Established a unified deploy staging architecture (outputs/site → website/sync_staging.py → Hugo build → deployment) Fixed corrupted PaperMod theme on macOS._pack files and updated to the latest version supporting Hugo v0.157.0 Generated 8 weekly reports for W05-W12 in batches Blocked Items:
⚠️ ROI for conference award recognition was extremely low (about 80% of batches returned empty, almost ineffective for specialized fields), replacement with external data sources is not completed CalendarPro (2 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Implemented BATCH_UPDATE full-link (semantic routing + keyword counting rules + LLM classifier + handler), fixed the core defect in batch task status reporting Keyword counting rules (2+ completion verbs → +0.30 boost) compensated for the blind spot of embedding similarity in multi-task messages Fixed GENERAL fallback so that substantial LLM responses were not discarded All 21 new tests + 72 related tests passed Blocked Items:
⚠️ The entire pytest suite hung due to HuggingFace semantic routing model download, marker isolation solution is not completed VLA Evaluation Assistant / CALVIN Format Conversion (2 active days) — ⏸️ Paused Completed:
Implemented real-time overlay of manip_progress values on evaluation videos (cv2.putText with white text and black outline) Fixed CALVIN RLDS → LeRoBot conversion script (dataset name, observation key names, directory coverage logic) Confirmed that failure in downloading RoboCasa MimicGen pre-trained data was an upstream issue, no code fixes needed Key Tasks- ✅ InteractionSegmenter轨迹分段架构重构（Contract全链路对齐） (2026-03-21) — Identified that inconsistencies in the contract across detector→injector→validator→generator stages are systematic root causes. The new InteractionSegmenter segments trajectories based on object interactions, with each segment having a clear target_object/phase/other_objects. This completely bypasses the缺陷 of single-object phase detection. Fixed semantic ambiguities for objects[0] in 13 skills, and ensured that target_object and target_pos are transmitted throughout the entire chain. All 139 unit tests passed. ✅ Error Recovery Pipeline全链路实现（8文件，139测试） (2026-03-16) — Implemented 8 new files including recovery_types.py/recovery_segmenter.py/collection augmentation transformation scripts, and added 34 unit tests. Completed the data collection plan for VLA error recovery (5 RBG groups, 6 tasks×3 tiers, total budget of 329 human demos). ✅ π₀.₅任务完成检测头设计与实现（训练上线） (2026-03-18) — Completed the entire chain: the source feature for prefix_output mean pooling, the inheritance method maintained compatibility with checkpoint paths, CompletionHead/Pi0WithCompletionHead/train_completion_head.py. Fixed the key bug where the alignment parameter was not injected into the evaluate stage in all_aligned mode. End-to-end verification showed that Harmony improved batch_entropy from 0.33 to 0.52. ✅ CalendarPro BATCH_UPDATE意图实现 (2026-03-18) — Modified 8 files to add BATCH_UPDATE enumeration, semantic routing (21 utterances), keyword counting rules (2+completed verbs→+0.30 boost), and LLM handler. Fixed GENERAL fallback issues. All 21 new tests plus 72 related tests passed. ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark三项核心bug修复 (2026-03-22) — Fixed the bug where Panda gripper action polarity was reversed (dynamically detected based on robot arm type), wrong_object filtering logic (only allowing grasp_geoms to be non-empty), and the injection strategy for drop_with_interaction (actively moving the object directly above the non-target and then releasing it). The number of training scenes increased from 1209 to 1627. ✅ gadget npx挂起bug修复 + ECC全员opus升级 (2026-03-21) — Fixed the bug where npx hung permanently in capture_output=True mode. Upgraded 27 agents from sonnet/haiku to opus, and changed settings.json’s effortLevel to max. ✅ 统一deploy staging架构建设 (2026-03-22) — Created common/site_staging.py to unify the output interface. All tools’ Hugo release paths were switched to outputs/site. Created website/sync_staging.py (priority given to link/copy rollback/first bootstrap migration). Added a complete release layer to benchmarks. update.sh integrated staging synchronization. ✅ robobrain_pi任务状态汇报bug修复 (2026-03-17) — Fixed 5 issues in vla_infer.py: infinite loops caused by not clearing current_prompt after the task was completed, potential crashes due to incorrect None check order, lack of broadcasting of idle states, and inconsistent debug log thresholds. ✅ gadget _finalized死循环根因诊断与修复 (2026-03-19) — Diagnosed the root cause of re-processing all historical dates each time in sync-all: some devices had source log _finalized=False on the day of the first export. When merging, all_sources_finalized=False caused report to never become finalized. Exactly identified 3 issues with source logs, and a manual re-export repair plan was determined. ✅ drop_in_transit假阳性诊断与修复 (2026-03-20) — Diagnosed that min_hold_height=0.85 led to misjudging objects on the table (z≈0.88) as being in the air (80% false positives). The threshold was raised to 0.93 (table height + object height + margin). D0 success rate increased from 10% to 50%. ✅ 训练场景并行批量生成（串行→32 worker） (2026-03-20) — Converted the serial script toThreadPoolExecutor (32 workers, 4 GPUs round-robin), completing 973 scenes in 41 minutes (about a 10-fold improvement). Subsequent multi-round generation eventually reached 1627 scenes. ✅ π₀.₅ manip_progress推理日志修复 (2026-03-18) — Identified and fixed the root cause: RobotwinOutputs.call only returned the {actions} field, silently discarding manip_progress, making the upstream prediction results completely invisible to external parties. The data flow was reconnected after modifying the output transform. ✅ MIHD批次效应评估指标纯Python实现 (2026-03-18) — Replaced the R package nabor with sklearn.neighbors.NearestNeighbors, and rewritten the ASW_batch/batch_entropy/batch_kl/graph_connectivity indicators in pure Python. 🔄 Pi0.5-base LoRA合并数据集微调流水线数据准备 (2026-03-20) — Completed data conversion from 5 tasks HDF5 to LeRoBot (about 8000 entries) and calculated norm stats. The coffee/stack training process was interrupted due to the expiration of the Slurm interactive job, so re-submission was required. The pick_place data generation also stopped when generating 184 out of 2000 entries. 🔄 three_piece_assembly退化根因分析 (2026-03-22) — Confirmed that Fix1-3 had been implemented. Identified two root causes not yet fixed: get_target_object() and InteractionSegmenter both selected targets from the all_objects with the closest distance. The base fixture never met the lift_height=0.84 threshold due to z≈0.80, resulting in 879 out of 887 opportunities being filtered out. The repair plan was clarified, and code implementation was scheduled for next week. ✅ Research Profiler消歧架构两轮重构 (2026-03-16) — Implemented a three-level parsing chain (exact ID→paper reverse search→name search), re-calibrated the weight of quantitative indicators in semantic disambiguation (h-index/number of papers significantly increased), added s2_author_id fields, –author-id CLI parameters, and S2 speed limit exponential backoff retries. Fixed issues where famous professors like Pieter Abbeel misidentified h-index. ✅ π₀.₅ Exp5-9实验配置设计与实现 (2026-03-18) — Added a cond_mode field to pi0_config.py, implementing Exp5-9: from_hidden+last/special_token, sinusoidal+last/special_token, detach_cond+last_token, five conditioning strategies. The system covered all three directions: end-to-end gradient, intermediate layer, and detached experiments. ✅ action_loss与task_loss分离监控 (2026-03-17) — Modified model.py’s abstract method to return (loss_array, aux_dict). train.py used JAX has_aux=True to unpack auxiliary indicators. Action_loss/task_loss independent curves were added in wandb and progress bars. ✅ VLA错误恢复数据采集方案设计（5 RBG分组） (2026-03-16) — Investigated literature such as MimicGen/IntervenGen, and clustered 29 error subtypes into 5 RBG groups (Re-grasp/Retrieve/Retract/Redirect/Realign). Established a total budget of 329 demos, saving 81% of the 1740 basic requirements. ✅ gpumon重复进程bug修复 (2026-03-17) — Dual filtering: required that both /dev/nvidia* FDs were open to be considered a GPU process (eliminating false positives in CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES environment variables), and added a parent process chain to remove duplicates (folding DDP worker child processes). The number of processes was reduced from 35 to 8. Added keyboard interaction navigation. ✅ 训练场景生成失败根因分析文档 (2026-03-20) — Analyzed through parallel_logs logs, opportunity map, and meta.json cross-analysis, identifying five major root causes (gripper_closed_norm with abnormal P0 level, insufficient drop collision detection, etc.), and documented in training_scene_failure_analysis.md. ✅ Hugo站点bugJournal导航下拉菜单实现 (2026-03-22) — Customized header.html partial to override the PaperMod default template, adding has-submenu/submenu class rendering logic, and implemented a hover dropdown effect through bugjournal-menu.css; list.html filtering ensured that only three subsections were displayed on the root page. Issues and Solutions 1. per-section PCA/STAIG generated non-compatable embedding spaces, resulting in 10 out of 14 combinations having SL@50=0, and normalize failed to fix [MIHD Space Transcriptomics] (2026-03-16) Solution: Switch to a pre-trained Foundation Model (scGPT): all slices share the same model weights, so the output is naturally in the same coordinate system. SL@50 increased from 0.013 to 0.416, with a 100% hit rate. The root cause was inconsistent coordinate systems (mathematically, normalize could not be resolved), rather than insufficient gene feature information.
2. CompositeBodyObject scattered during env.reset() (lid floating, base offset), but normal when replayed via HDF5 (set_sim_state_flat covered the body position to mask the issue) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-16) Solution: Added a locations_relative_to_corner instance attribute in init of generated_objects.py to restore the coordinate transformation logic from the corner to the center (aligned with commit 398af01b). All 139 unit tests passed.
3. Semantic Scholar mistakenly combined papers by multiple researchers with the same name, and famous professors like Pieter Abbeel were matched to the same person with h=4, causing serious distortion in h-index statistics [Research Profiler] (2026-03-16) Solution: Reconstructed the disambiguation weights (quantitative indicators overpower string similarity), added a three-level parsing chain (exact ID→paper reverse search→name search), and –author-id CLI parameters. During speed limits, the correct authorId was found through WebSearch and directly specified.
4. vla_infer.py did not clear the current_prompt after the task was completed. If the chunk_count in the next loop was still ≥5 and the model score was high, it would immediately trigger a done message, causing an infinite loop reporting completion [robobrain_pi] (2026-03-17) Solution: Added current_prompt=’’ and publish_state(‘idle’) at the end of the task completion processing block. The state machine must reset both the triggering conditions and broadcast the state change upon completion.
5. In π₀.₅ Pi0WithCompletionHead, using a combination method caused all parent module parameter paths to have a pi0 prefix. pytree merge threw a ‘2 children vs 1 child’ ValueError, and checkpoints could not be loaded at all [π₀.₅] (2026-03-18) Solution: Changed to inheritance (class Pi0WithCompletionHead(Pi0)), and Pi0 parameters were directly aligned with the checkpoint path at the top level. Inheritance in Flax NNX was the only way to maintain compatibility with checkpoints.
6. RobotwinOutputs.call only returned the {actions} field, silently discarding manip_progress. Even if the model correctly predicted the progress field, it could never be obtained externally, and the eval txt file was always empty [π₀.₅] (2026-03-18) Solution: Modified the output transform’s return dict to include the manip_progress field. The data flow was reconnected. Such bugs should be traced back from the end of the data flow.
7. When CalendarPro users reported task status, the system returned ‘I’m not sure’ instead of processing the request [CalendarPro] (2026-03-18) Solution: Analyzed the entire root cause chain: low embedding similarity → LLM classifier failed to classify this intent → GENERAL handler discarded an AI response. Added BATCH_UPDATE support in the routing/classification/processing triple-chain, and keyword counting rules compensated for the embedding blind spot.
8. In MIHD all_aligned mode, the –alignment parameter was not injected into the evaluate stage. EvaluationJob.alignment remained None, and the Harmony alignment result was completely ignored, making the alignment function ineffective [MIHD Space Transcriptomics] (2026-03-18) Solution: Added three lines of code before the run_pipeline.py evaluate stage: when phase==‘all_aligned’ and args.alignment had a value, inject the alignment into the extra_config of each experiment.
9. Drop_in_transit_D0 only generated very few valid scenarios. The min_hold_height=0.85 was too low. Objects on the table (z≈0.88) were misjudged as being in the air, resulting in 80% false positives [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-20) Solution: Increased the threshold to 0.93 (table height 0.80 + object height 0.08 + margin). False positives were eliminated, and the D0 success rate increased from 10% to 50%.
10. The python summarize/daily_summary.py remained stuck in @ccusage/codex steps, with no output or error messages [gadget] (2026-03-21) Solution: In subprocess.run(capture_output=True), redirected the stdin toDEVNULL. For npx installations, it waited indefinitely for the installation confirmation prompt. Added –yes parameter to skip the interaction confirmation in all 3 npx calls.
11. In multi-object tasks like threading/pick_place, phase detection failed systematically. All frames in threading were marked as pre_reach, resulting in 12/13 skills being unable to find opportunities [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-21) Solution: Added InteractionSegmenter to segment trajectories based on object interactions (EEF proximity + gripper state + co-motion detection). Each segment had a clear target_object and phase. Completely bypassed single-object phase detection, not repairing the defect but abstracting it. The threading subtypes were reduced from 3 to 25.
12. Panda’s mechanical arm action polarity was opposite to the code assumption: Panda required action=+1 for closing, while the hard-coded -1 actually opened the gripper, resulting in all injection failures for coffee/stack and others [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-22)Solution: In EnvWrapper.init, detect the polarity based on the robotic arm type. Set close_action to +1.0 for PandaGripper and -1.0 for RethinkGripper. Add a helper function to replace all hardcoded action values [-1]. 13. drop_with_interaction in all task generation scenarios: The original implementation only applied a displacement of 1-3cm before releasing the object, resulting in objects not touching their neighbors at all when the distance between them was 10-20cm [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-22) Solution: Rewrite the inject logic. Move the object directly above the non-target object (+0.15m), then release the gripper to let the object fall freely. Track the object-object contact during the settle process.
Learning Outcomes Architecture Pipeline contracts must track the entire data flow: not only should the processing logic be modified, but the data must also be persisted at the storage level (e.g., results of segment_interactions() are written to NPZ). The output transform is an implicit filter in the reasoning chain, and any fields that don’t appear in return dict are silently discarded. In a multi-stage pipeline, CLI parameters must be explicitly passed to each downstream stage, not relying on implicit sharing. “Passing unit tests doesn’t mean E2E correctness” In multi-object robot operations tasks, target_object must be transmitted through the detector/injector/validator stages consistently. Objects[0] is the first object in dictionary insertion order. This approach leads to systematic errors when the semantics of multi-object scenarios are completely incorrect and no errors are reported. Graspable objects and fixtures must be distinguished by the grasp_geoms field, and targets cannot be selected based solely on distance/position. Independent processing per section (PCA/training) results in non-comparable embedding spaces, which is a fundamental limitation at the architecture level. This cannot be fixed through normalization or post-processing. The only correct zero-shot solution is to pre-train a Foundation Model (shared weights). Gene FM (scGPT 100%) outperforms Vision FM (UNI2 71%) because gene expression has stronger cross-sample consistency due to the cross-sample effect in H&E images. Flax NNX extended models: Inheriting (class Child(Parent)) is the only way to maintain compatibility with checkpoint paths. Combining (self.pi0 = Pi0(…) adds a prefix before all parent module parameter paths, causing pytree merge to fail completely. The existing framework’s freezing mechanism (freeze_filter+nnx.DiffState) usually takes scalability into account and should be reused rather than creating new ones. When facing architectural design flaws, “bypassing” is more fundamental than “patching”: The assumption of phase detection for single objects is a design flaw that cannot be fixed through parameter tuning. InteractionSegmenter bypasses it directly instead of patching it, which is a more elegant solution. Instead of patching, we bypass the wrong abstraction layer. Debugging Principles of state machine design: Completing an event must involve two actions simultaneously—resetting the trigger conditions (clearing current_prompt) and broadcasting state changes (_publish_state(‘idle’)). Doing either one alone will lead to infinite loops or implicit bugs where upstream perception fails. The None check must occur before any property access (defensive programming). When diagnosing behavior of unknown third-party libraries, writing a minimal test script is more reliable and efficient than static code analysis (e.g., in the case of npx gripper polarity). Logging Counter statistics is more effective at identifying systematic bugs than analyzing each bug individually (1698 occurrences of ‘gripper not closed’ directly pointing to target_object ambiguity). To troubleshoot implicit filtering class bugs, track back from the end of the data flow. Experimental naming conventions must strictly correspond to the actual implementation. Using inconsistent names like “pca_uni2_staig_fusion” can lead to long-term misunderstandings. It’s necessary to confirm the actual configuration by reading logs rather than just looking at the experimental name. Quality issues with benchmark data (uneven distribution, all phase annotations are pre_reach) should be monitored during pipeline design. Tools subprocess.run(capture_output=True) redirects stdin to DEVNULL. When calling CLI tools that may have interactive prompts (npx), it’s necessary to specify –yes/-y, otherwise the process will hang indefinitely instead of timing out. capture_output=True is the root cause of high-secrets hanging. The pam_slurm_adopt policy in Slurm HPC clusters forces killing all related processes when a job ends. SSH nohup cannot bypass this. Long-training tasks must be submitted as official jobs using sbatch. MuJoCo physics simulation (enable_camera=False) is a pure CPU task. MUJOCO_GL=disabled can completely bypass OpenGL. The number of workers should exactly match –cpus-per-task. JAX has_aux mechanism: nnx.value_and_grad supports has_aux=True. After compute_loss returns(loss_array, aux_dict), the gradient is calculated only for loss_array. Aux_dict transparently carries monitoring indicators. stop_gradient restricts aux loss to MLP parameters strictly. The ratio of action/aux loss values doesn’t indicate training imbalance. Hugo best practices: .Title is the most stable field for identifying specific sections (the case sensitivity of .Section and .RelPermalink differs across platforms). Using the same static directory name as the basic name of the content page can cause path conflicts. Automatic frontmatter generation shouldn’t use the current system time as the date. The theme partial can be safely overridden through project-level layouts/partials without having to fork the theme. Spatial data nearest neighbor query: For large point clouds (>10K points), KD trees should be used first (O(N log N) preprocessing), rather than cdist (O(N²) memory). In HD spatial transcriptomics scenarios with 17K+ cells, differences can reach OOM instead of normal operation. The principle of single truth source (SSOT): Constant sets that need to be referenced across multiple files must be imported from the only definable place. Domain Knowledge Recovery Behavior Group (RBG) grouping strategy: Cluster 29 error subtypes into 5 groups based on motor primitives. Demos within the same group can be cross-enhanced. MimicGen generates over 1000 demos from 10 source demos. Compress 1740 simple requirements into 329 requirements (saving 81%). This is a paradigm that can be widely applied to robot recovery data engineering. π₀.₅ calculation graph characteristics: Reasoning naturally involves two steps (VLM prefix forward → action expert denoising). During training, Exp1-4 can be combined into one joint forward using teacher forcing. The from_hidden mode introduces circular dependencies due to conditioning dependent on model output. An additional prefix-only forward is necessary. The mean pooling of prefix_output is more suitable for classification tasks than the suffix_output of action expert (encoding global state understanding). LLMs have a deeper understanding of academic trajectory analysis than expected: They can automatically identify higher-order features such as “infrastructure thinking researchers” and “technical prerequisites for paradigm shifts”. The why_not_before field (the historical prerequisites for each breakthrough in terms of data/computing power/insight) is the most valuable field in researcher profiling. Identifying conference awards has very low ROI under current LLM capabilities. It should be replaced by an external data source solution (exact title matching JSON library). Notes on AI Usage Effective Modes:
✓ Parallel multi-agent code review: Analyze the same diff from three dimensions of reuse/quality/efficiency independently. This reveals completely overlapping problems across three dimensions, and parallel execution saves time. ✓ Systematically track the root cause of code: Track back from the end of the data flow to identify bugs in the output transform that silently discard bugs, S2 rate-limiting recursive loops, etc. ✓ Proactive quality checks beyond task boundaries: Actively identify confusion due to same names in researcher profiling (abnormal ratios between h-index and number of papers, impossible domain spans), which are not explicitly required in prompts. ✓ Academic trajectory analysis reaches an academic review level: Automatically identifies higher-order features such as “infrastructure thinking researchers”. The why_not_before field has more value than expected. ✓ Log aggregation statistics to identify systematic bugs: Counter statistics show that 1698 occurrences of ‘gripper not closed’ directly point to target_object ambiguity root causes. This is more efficient than analyzing each bug individually. Limitations:
✗ When dealing with complex system problems, we tend to focus on identifying isolated bugs. We lack the ability to analyze contract inconsistencies from a full data flow perspective and need human guidance at the architectural level. ✗ After completing local repair plans, we lack proactive global data flow verification. Passing unit tests gives us a false sense of completion. Many omissions (e05/e06/e09, target_pos transmission, collect script not integrated) need to be identified through systematic reviews by humans. ✗ Environmental configurations have not been pre-verified, resulting in multiple full failures for batch tasks (Agent sub-proxies lack Bash permissions, conda activation fails, PYTHONPATH is missing, API key type). We should verify them individually before expanding into batches. ✗ We failed to actively identify efficiency bottlenecks: When serial scenario generation was slow, we only set waiting conditions without considering parallelization. No consideration was given to resource isolation when nohup SSH starts and Slurm jobs expire. ✗ We didn’t anticipate specific framework behaviors: We didn’t foresee the impact of Flax NNX inheritance vs combination on checkpoint paths. We didn’t anticipate that JAX/NNX TrainConfig mechanisms can be directly reused. ✗ We don’t understand the latest features of tools (effortLevel: only known after being informed). We have knowledge gaps in the rapidly evolving AI tool ecosystem. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core priorities are: (1) Complete the Error Recovery Benchmark—fix three_piece_assembly by prioritizing get_target_object and InteractionSegmenter to get_graspable_objects() first, with all objects being returned to their original state. The goal is to reduce the number of task subtypes from 4 to 20+. Complete the collect script to call segment_interactions() so that segmented data can be persisted in NPZ; (2) Restart π₀.₅ training—submit six tasks of Pi0.5-base LoRA combined fine-tuning and training using sbatch. Complete the generation of pick_place data (2000 D0 instances). Monitor the comparison of action_loss/task_loss curves for Exp1-4 and Exp5-9; (3) Advance MIHD research—start writing papers on cross-slice analysis based on scGPT’s zero-shot advantage established this week. Integrate Harmony alignment results (batch_entropy 0.52) as a baseline; (4) Release the third version of Research Profiler—implement paper title search support (planned but not implemented this week). Replace conference award identification with an external Best Paper JSON database solution; (5) Stabilize infrastructure—promote the completed unified staging architecture to all tools in research/summarize/benchmark. Verify that sync.py performs normally for all categories.

summary: This week, we worked across three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. We conducted comprehensive research in two key areas: robotic operations and spatial transcriptomics. The Error Recovery Benchmark project was completed from design of the collection scheme (329 demo cases for RBG grouping) to architectural-level trajectory segmentation using InteractionSegmenter. In total, 1,627 training scenarios were generated, covering 148 subtypes, representing a 35% increase in data coverage. MIHD spatial transcriptomics further improved by integrating cross-slice embedding techniques, establishing scGPT’s zero-shot advantage with a 100% accuracy rate compared to UNI2’s 71%. For π₀.₅, we completed the full-chain training of the detection head, achieving an error rate of approximately 0.253. We also developed five conditioning strategies for Exp5-9 scenarios. The gadget toolchain was upgraded toopus+max thinking, with improvements in Research Profiler architecture, unified deployment of websites, and full upgrades for ECC devices. This week’s key breakthroughs focused on “identifying and resolving root causes at the architectural level”: issues such as non-comparability between per-section PCA coordinate systems, ambiguity in target_object classification for multiple objects, and compatibility between Flax NNX inheritance and combination techniques were all fundamentally resolved this week.
